rows.note: deterministic round-robin shared-counter stepping is admitted only because schedule order and memory ordering are frozen explicitly
deterministic barrier release plus reduce stays inside the research envelope when barrier order is fixed and replayable
host-owned scheduler selection remains refused because replay and challenge receipts would collapse without a frozen scheduler trace
relaxed memory ordering remains refused because the deterministic scheduler envelope does not rescue weak-order semantics

world_mount_dependency_marker: world-mounts remain the authority owner for task-scoped shared-memory and scheduler admission outside standalone psionic
kernel_policy_dependency_marker: kernel-policy remains the authority owner for settlement-grade scheduler and side-effect admission outside standalone psionic
claim_boundary: this sandbox boundary admits only named deterministic scheduler shapes for the research threads profile and refuses host-nondeterministic scheduling plus relaxed shared-memory ordering. It does not create a served threads lane, arbitrary shared-memory portability, or general concurrency closure
summary: Threads scheduler sandbox boundary report freezes 2 allowed deterministic cases and 2 refused out-of-envelope cases.
